entry.title: Washington Post: 24 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Ukraine to withdraw troops from Severodonetsk
  • In many countries, abortion is protected by law, not court decision
  • U.N. rights body says Israeli soldiers killed American journalist in West Bank
  • Boris Johnson’s Tories suffer fresh electoral defeat; party chair quits
  • Ukraine retreats from Severodonetsk as Russia advances in the east
  • Britain to pull $18B in bank notes as it switches from paper to polymer
  • They wanted rule of law, so China tried them in secret
  • Kyiv granted E.U. membership candidacy in symbolic win amid war
  • Russia targeted Ukrainian ammunition to weaken Kyiv on the battlefield
  • Afghanistan endures yet another deadly shock
  • WHO weighs declaring monkeypox a global emergency as European cases surge
  • Sri Lanka’s economy has ‘completely collapsed,’ prime minister says
  • E.U. has backed candidate status for Ukraine. Here’s what that means.
  • E.U. leaders make Ukraine a candidate for membership
  • Traces of highly contagious poliovirus found in British sewage
  • U.S. monkeypox response mirrors early coronavirus missteps, experts say
  • Rights group slams Palestinian Authority over activist death
  • Turkey wildfire largely under control, Erdogan says
  • Vatican to start monthly newspaper by and with the poor
  • Indigenous and family mourn expert killed in the Amazon
  • Afghan national freed from Guantanamo Bay after 15 years
  • Ukrainian army to leave battered city to avoid encirclement
  • UN office points to Israel in Al Jazeera reporter's death
  • Newcomer ousts longtime prime minister in Grenada elections
  • Woman who had miscarriage in Malta taken to Spain to abort
  • Live updates: Supreme Court overturns Roe v. Wade, ending constitutional protection for abortion
  • Crowd at Supreme Court explodes with outrage, joy as Roe v. Wade falls
  • Tex. school district bans dresses, skirts to promote ‘workforce skills’
  • Roe’s demise marks new phase in state-by-state battle over abortion
  • Tell The Post: How do you feel about the Supreme Court’s abortion ruling?
  • ‘Still a nightmare’: Surfside families mark one year since collapse
  • 6,000 bees moved into a century-old home. Savvy beekeepers evicted them.
  • J.K. Rowling thought she was talking to Zelensky. It was Russian pranksters.
  • They went hiking to get closer to God. Instead, they had to be rescued.
  • Jan. 6 committee, DOJ seek footage of Roger Stone from Danish filmmakers
  • She ordered a burger on Grubhub — and included a desperate plea for help
  • Publix won’t offer coronavirus vaccines to children under 5 ‘at this time’
  • Post Politics Now: House poised to send gun legislation to Biden in response to mass shootings
  • Intelligence contractor, alleged Nazi sympathizer charged in Jan. 6 riot
  • Echoes of Watergate: Trump’s appointees reveal his push to topple Justice Dept.
  • Rep. Scott Perry played key role in promoting false claims of fraud
  • Jan. 6 panel names five Republicans who allegedly sought Trump pardons
  • Home of Jeffrey Clark, Trump DOJ official, searched by federal agents
  • Jan. 6 hearing recap: Trump White House aides say multiple GOP members of Congress asked for pardons
  • U.S. broadens Jan. 6 seditious conspiracy indictment of Oath Keepers
  • As Biden touts gas tax pause, even some of his own officials balk
  • How women’s lives were different before Roe v. Wade
  • Crypto hackers steal $100 million from blockchain bridge
  • Stocks rally, positioning Wall Street to end week on high note
  • This tax season, IRS answered just 10 percent of taxpayer calls
  • What is a recession? Your economy questions, answered.
  • How to scrub yourself from the internet, the best that you can
  • Earthquake tech could limit deaths. Afghanistan shows it’s not easy.
  • Multigenerational, ADA-compliant house lists for $2.2M
  • The UK’s Rail Strike Is a Warning for the US Labor Movement
  • A Reckless Ruling, a New Gun-Safety Challenge
  • Faster Progress Is Needed on Treatments for Long Covid
  • Supreme Court’s New York Harbor Case Isn’t a ‘Sopranos’ Episode
  • Why Billionaires Are Flinging Satellites Into ‘LEO’: QuickTake
  • Understanding the Juul Ban and Concerns About Teen Vaping
  • Polio Virus Found in London Sewage: Should the UK Worry?
  • Here’s How US States Differ on Carrying Guns in Public
  • For the fall of Roe v. Wade, thank Donald Trump
  • Will Biden run for president again? 12 of our columnists have the answer.
  • We know what to do to save the giant sequoias
  • Happy the elephant had her day in court. We humans are better for it.
  • Europe’s youngest democracy is a new front in the U.S.-Russia battle of ideas
  • Why France’s overseas territories are voting for anyone but Macron
  • Far too little has changed in Md. since our first gun violence protest
  • End the tipped wage to help back-of-house staff
  • Now is the time for moral courage, not more guns
  • Protect the Potomac
  • Protecting speech and ensuring safety in Virginia after Dobbs
  • Readers critique The Post: Photos didn’t show the whole picture
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Who is to blame for overturning Roe? Jennifer Rubin answers your questions.
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times: 24 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

  • States Rush to Revamp Laws After Supreme Court’s Gun Ruling

  • ‘Ghost Flights’? The Facts Behind Transporting Migrant Children
  • Following Recall, San Francisco School Board Reverses Course
  • U.S. Repatriates Afghan Whose Guantánamo Detention Was Unlawful
  • Experimental Psychedelic Therapy Returns to the V.A.
  • With Swag and Swagger, State Democrats Vie for Front of Presidential Primary Line
  • Which Republican Senators Voted for the Gun Control Bill?
  • Pelosi’s Husband Charged With D.U.I. in California Car Crash

  • To Catch a Snake: Largest Python Found in Everglades Signals a Threat
  • White House Official Says He’s Still Optimistic About Covid Aid Despite G.O.P. Objections
  • Eric Greitens to Face New G.O.P. Attacks in Missouri Senate Race

  • In Bronx Housing Court, Tenants Fight to Stay in Their Homes
  • As Midterms Loom, Mark Zuckerberg Shifts Focus Away From Elections
  • Government to Cancel $6 Billion in Student Loans for Defrauded Borrowers
  • The Rise of the 0.5 Selfie
  • As Russia Chokes Ukraine’s Grain Exports, Romania Tries to Fill In
  • Stocks Regain Ground, Set For a Rare Weekly Gain

entry.title: Guardian: 24 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Former Tory leader Michael Howard says Boris Johnson should resign after double byelection loss – UK politics live
  • Russia-Ukraine war: Kyiv orders troops to pull out of Sievierodonetsk as fears grow Lysychansk also set to fall – live
  • England v New Zealand: third Test, day two – live!
  • Roe v Wade overturned as supreme court strikes down federal right to abortion – live

  • Murdoch’s divorce will leave a hole in his life. Could a new prime minister fill it? | Marina Hyde
  • Nasa halts auction of moon dust and cockroaches expected to sell for $400,000

  • Fears grow for Afghanistan earthquake survivors left without shelter
  • A planet in peril and our embrace of Big Brother: George Orwell would have been shocked | Rebecca Solnit
  • Wimbledon 2022: Raducanu gets tough draw as Murray faces Duckworth opener
  • ‘Those bastard developments’ – why the inventor of the shopping mall denounced his creation
  • Charles tells Commonwealth leaders dropping Queen is ‘for each to decide’
  • Liverpool and Manchester United heading for Bangkok – at a big cost
  • German gas prices could triple as Russia reduces supply, expert says
  • Foetus fronts legal challenge over emissions in South Korea
  • The US supreme court voted in favour of … people getting shot | Hamilton Nolan
  • Phoebe Bridgers on Taylor Swift, Paul Mescal and why she spoke about her abortion: ‘I wasn’t emotional at all’
  • Chinese premier calls for more coal production as electricity demand soars
  • Two killed after Chinese electric car falls from third floor of company’s offices
  • What do the byelection results mean for the Conservatives? Our panel’s verdict | David Gauke and others

  • Experience: I lost my sight overnight
  • Man vs Bee review – Rowan Atkinson channels Bean and Baldrick in his new slapstick sitcom
  • Why the west risks condemning Ukraine to slow strangulation
  • ‘He died in his 30s living the life he had dreamed of’: artist Eric Ravilious
  • Trump feeling fallout of Capitol attack hearings as allies abandon ship

  • Paolo Banchero is surprise No 1 overall pick for Magic in NBA draft

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 24 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 24 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-24 14:46PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Cracks in first-floor flat hanging above passers-by in Hong Kong spark calls for inspection of nearby structures
  • First phase of new cable works completed, Hong Kong power firm says, days after fire from high-voltage lines caused widespread blackouts
  • Hong Kong Book Fair to begin on July 20 but some publishers banned with ‘no reason’
  • Hong Kong court orders European firm to pay HK$150,000, apologise to former employee sacked over sexual harassment claim
  • Hong Kong customs officers arrest 5 men after seizure of HK$4.5 million worth of frozen meat from ship
  • Nansha tech boost offers a bigger cake for ‘brother’ Hong Kong, not rivalry: Guangzhou mayor
  • Hong Kong chauffeur acquitted on charge of inciting members of drivers’ WhatsApp group to attack police with machetes
  • Why Hong Kong’s new textbooks for secondary schools have stopped calling the city a British colony

  • Passing on Lessons from the Field
  • Discovering a Passion for Literature
  • Immigration: the Dream vs the Facts

  • Covid-19 in China: Woman and ailing father threatened with detention after clash with police while trying to visit hospital
  • Chinese firm aiming to break US dominance in chip design software gets IPO approval at home
  • China, US shipping rates continue to tumble as inflation cools consumer demand
  • China’s internet watchdog launches cybersecurity investigation into country’s largest academic research database CNKI
  • China’s new live-streaming guidelines set to change the influencer business known for its ‘low threshold, high income’
  • ‘See you next year’: China’s ‘King of gaokao’ fails to get marks for dream university after sitting the entrance exam for 26th time
  • Chinese security official calls for crackdown on gangs following Tangshan attack
  • China’s 20th party congress: former political star Lu Hao named to head key policy think tank
  • Second Chinese rights activist Ding Jiaxi stands trial on state subversion charges in secret proceeding
  • Video: China’s Chang’e 5 lunar lander finds water on the moon, but not as much as they hoped
  • Video: China to send rocket on collision course with asteroid to knock it into safer orbit
  • Business groups urge Biden to return trade to heart of Asia policies
  • LGBT rights in China: transgender youth seeking gender affirming surgery find family approval still biggest hurdle
  • China, South Korea battle population woes as ‘children are not a must’, adding to economic peril
  • China’s world’s factory tag threatened by Vietnam, but ‘there’s nothing to worry about’, analysts say
  • Why China should brace itself for a Pacific rebuff
  • Chinese radar breakthrough will see commercial drones soar in power and applications, says Shenzhen team
  • Malaysia 1MDB scandal: Blow for Najib as court allows co-accused Arul Kanda to testify against him in trial
  • Myanmar’s NUG says junta placed Aung San Suu Kyi in solitary confinement to ‘embarrass’ her
  • China’s ambassador urges new Australian government to repair ties
  • Birthday giveaway: Richest Asian Gautam Adani pledges US$7.7 billion for social causes
  • North Korea approves ‘stronger war deterrent’ at key military meeting
  • Can Indonesia’s Jokowi breathe easy, with air pollution in Jakarta and other cities still causing tens of thousands of premature deaths?
  • Myanmar’s Suu Kyi moved to solitary confinement in prison, but said to be ‘in strong spirits’
  • US Supreme Court overturns Roe vs Wade, ending constitutional right to abortion
  • Prince Charles says Commonwealth nations free to decide on dropping queen as head of state
  • UN says Al Jazeera journalist Shireen Abu Akleh killed by Israeli forces
  • Ukraine forces to retreat from battleground city Sievierodonetsk to avoid encirclement
  • World leaders seek united front for Ukraine at key summits as war rages
  • Balkans complain about stalled EU bids as war-torn Ukraine gets candidate status
  • Covid-19 vaccines ‘saved 20 million lives’ in first year, study says
  • UK’s Boris Johnson suffers double by-election defeat humiliation
  • Pink Floyd seeking US$500 million for music catalogue
  • UAE sells US$3 billion in high-quality debt amid recession fears
  • US sending Ukraine US$450 million more in arms, including rocket systems, says White House
  • Bill Cosby to appeal civil ruling on teen sex assault
  • Nike to make full exit from Russia due to war in Ukraine
  • European Union agrees to make Ukraine a candidate for EU membership
  • US Senate passes gun safety bill as Supreme Court makes it easier for Americans to carry handguns
  • British Airways set for ‘summer of strikes’ at London’s Heathrow
  • H&M closes Shanghai flagship store following lockdowns and consumer backlash
  • 33-year-old Chinese woman splashes US$84.2 million in cash on exclusive 49-room property in Sardinia
  • Hong Kong’s second SPAC zooms in on smart cars, supply chain, e-commerce as potential takeover targets
  • ‘Death of Hong Kong’ predictions were wrong in 1997 and are still off the mark today
  • CATL to supply Li Auto, Hozon electric car battery with a range of 1,000km as Chinese firm seeks to stay on top
  • Hong Kong billionaire Henry Cheng’s family aims to take control of apparel retailer Giordano
  • Alibaba, XPeng drive Hong Kong stocks to two-week high on China recovery outlook, Xi pledge
  • GogoX slumps in trading debut as Hong Kong’s first unicorn marks the return of IPO ceremonial gong since pandemic
  • UBS’s top China banker says Hong Kong still a magnet for global IPOs, with additional reforms likely to attract more start-ups in Greater Bay Area
  • Two dead after Nio car falls from third floor of headquarters building in Shanghai during test drive
  • HKEX’s London Metal Exchange names Oliver Wyman to review nickel trading chaos amid investor lawsuits
  • Look to portfolio resilience in times of volatility
  • Talent flourishes in Hong Kong’s vibrant I&T ecosystem, with global and diverse minds developing careers in the city
  • Apple and Android phones hacked by Italian spyware, Google says
  • CityU students’ blockchain-powered solution for used car buyer protection wins bronze in the Fintech Olympiad 2022
  • How to manage teenage acne: simple skincare is key, says expert, as is sunscreen and consistency
  • Down in one: the origin of shooters, how they and their non-PC names are still part of bar culture, and where to get them in Hong Kong
  • The risks and benefits of diet trends on Instagram and TikTok, as explained by an expert
  • Best new Hong Kong restaurants: Italian, French Japanese, fusion – fresh concepts from the King of Truffles, the Demon Chef, and more
  • How Hong Kong developed a world-leading port from the ashes of war, and factories, and its maritime future
  • Indonesia, Portugal and South Africa mull digital-nomad visas, but how to define exactly what ‘work’ is and where it is happening?
  • Welcomed with leis and ukuleles on an extraordinary South Pacific cruise by cargo ship from Tahiti, tourists revel in Raivavae, Rapa and the other Austral Islands of French Polynesia
  • VELA CASA showcases charismatic, art-level European designer brand furniture
  • Who’s the richest Presley? Elvis and family’s net worths, ranked: from Priscilla’s Graceland millions and Lisa-Marie’s tax debts, to Caitlyn Jenner’s ex Linda Thompson and final fiancée Ginger Alden
  • Meet Jennifer Lopez’s teen son, Max Muñiz: his twin sibling Emme, who came out as queer this week, is a singer – but he’s already made his acting debut and bonds with Ben Affleck over films
  • Do prohibitive ‘foreigner fees’ really keep expats out? Singapore slapped a 35 per cent stamp duty on overseas property investors – Bali, Canada, Thailand and New Zealand all blocked international buyers
  • Inside Matthew Williams’ first Givenchy menswear show at Paris Men’s Fashion Week: his spring/summer 2023 collection channelled NYC street style – but was it high fashion enough for the runway?
  • 7 K-pop stars openly supporting the LGBT community, from Red Velvet’s Yeri, Girls’ Generation’s Tiffany, 2NE1’s CL and Le Sserafim’s Yunjin – who posted a throwback pic to celebrate Pride Month
  • Who is Titus Low, Singapore’s controversial influencer? His OnlyFans page landed him in hot water, but he now drives an AMG Mercedes, launched his own line of NFTs and might’ve married Cheryl Chin
  • Meet Samuel Chatto, Queen Elizabeth’s heartthrob grandnephew: the British royal is 29th in line to the throne, a pottery artist and qualified yoga instructor who trained in India
  • Meet Jude Law’s mirror-image son Rafferty – from modelling for Dolce & Gabbana and campaigning for PETA, to acting in Masters of the Air with Austin Butler, he could become just as famous as his dad
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 24 Jun, 2022
entry.content:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
